high.title: Oil Price Surge Hammers Global Bond Markets
high.summary: Global bond markets suffered severe losses this week as oil prices surged from $72/barrel pre-war to $92/barrel, with European natural gas prices soaring. UK 10-year gilt yields jumped 39bp to 4.62% - the worst week since the 2022 Truss crisis, while US 10-year Treasury yields rose 17bp to 4.13%. The Bloomberg Global Bond Index posted its worst week since October 2024. Investors fear inflation resurgence will derail central bank easing cycles. Market expectations have shifted dramatically: the ECB is now expected to raise rates 25bp this year versus pre-war expectations of further cuts. Bank of England rate cut bets fell from 50bp to just 50% chance of 25bp cut. However, US bonds performed relatively better given America's energy exporter status, and February's 92,000 job decline boosted Fed easing expectations. This energy-driven inflation shock poses significant risks to Taiwan's life insurers' bond portfolios and could delay CBC monetary easing.

high.title: Hon Hai Targets Double-Digit Growth, Promises NVIDIA Gift at GTC
high.summary: Hon Hai Chairman Liu Yang-wei announced the company expects "definite double-digit growth" in 2026 revenue, driven by dual engines of AI servers and consumer electronics recovery. With 2025 revenue of NT$8 trillion, double-digit growth would push toward NT$9 trillion target. The company secured major wins including Apple and Google foldable phone orders, low-cost iPhone 17e production, and largest supplier status for H2 iPhone 18 series. Liu expressed optimism about NVIDIA's Vera Rubin AI server platform, citing Hon Hai's deep client relationships and vertical integration advantages. The company will showcase Vera Rubin-related products at NVIDIA's GTC conference mid-March, including AI server racks, compute trays, cooling systems, and power components. Liu teased a "special gift" for NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang related to Vera Rubin platform. Strong order momentum and shipment dynamics support the growth outlook.

high.title: TSMC Launches Massive Recruitment Drive for 8,000 New Hires
high.summary: TSMC announced its 2026 campus recruitment campaign, targeting 8,000 new employees across Taiwan locations including Taoyuan, Hsinchu, Miaoli, Taichung, Chiayi, Tainan, and Kaohsiung. The semiconductor giant is expanding beyond traditional engineering roles to recruit business administration, HR, and accounting professionals. Fresh master's degree graduates can expect average annual compensation of NT$2.2 million. TSMC emphasized its digital transformation and AI/big data initiatives for next-generation smart factories, specifically welcoming IT talent. The company will participate in 17 physical campus job fairs and five online sessions. TSMC's DNA summer internship program also launched, targeting junior-year students with potential for full-time pre-offers. This massive hiring drive reflects TSMC's confidence in sustained semiconductor demand growth and positions the company to capture expanding AI chip opportunities while maintaining manufacturing excellence leadership.

medium.title: Oracle and OpenAI Abandon Texas Data Center Expansion
medium.summary: Oracle and OpenAI decided against expanding their largest data center complex in Abilene, Texas, impacting NVIDIA's next-generation Vera Rubin chip deployment strategy. The original eight-building plan for 400,000 NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs continues, but the additional six-building expansion was scrapped. Power infrastructure delays and OpenAI's preference for deploying next-gen Vera Rubin chips at other locations drove the decision. NVIDIA is reportedly helping lease remaining space to Meta, though negotiations remain preliminary.

medium.title: Shipping Rates Surge 11% Weekly as Middle East Crisis Disrupts Global Routes
medium.summary: The Shanghai Containerized Freight Index (SCFI) jumped 11.7% week-on-week to 1,489.19 points, with Asia-Persian Gulf rates surging 72% to $2,287/TEU amid US-Iran conflict. Shipping lines avoid Middle East ports despite operational status, creating vessel queues and reducing capacity by at least 10%. Trans-Pacific and Europe routes also posted gains of 1-4.5% as supply tightens during traditionally weak post-Lunar New Year period.

medium.title: Plastics Group Expects Q2 Recovery on Energy Price Rally
medium.summary: Formosa Plastics Group expects Q2 operations to improve gradually, supported by Middle East conflict-driven energy and petrochemical price increases plus strong AI electronic materials demand. Taiwan Plastics noted China's export tax rebate cancellations prompted front-loading purchases, while Nan Ya highlighted continued AI server demand boosting copper foil substrate, copper foil, and glass fiber products. The group sees potential for petrochemical price increases as Asian refineries undergo maintenance.

medium.title: Women Hold 16% of Global Gold Reserves, Benefiting from Price Rally
medium.summary: Julius Baer research reveals women, particularly in India, are major beneficiaries of gold's price surge, holding an estimated 16% of global gold reserves. Female participation in gold investing tripled between 2018-2024, reaching 26% at Royal Mint. Chinese middle-aged female investors account for one-third of gold bar and coin purchases. The trend reflects women's preference for conservative investments offering stability and diversification during uncertain periods.

medium.title: Smart Network Achieves Record February Revenue
medium.summary: Accton Technology posted February revenue of NT$23.58 billion, up 9.6% monthly and 82.7% annually, marking the strongest February on record. Foreign brokers raised price target to NT$1,850 from NT$1,620, citing continued 800G product shipments and new hyperscaler client contributions expected to reach 15% of revenue. Q1 revenue expected to decline mid-to-high single digits quarterly but grow ~60% annually.

medium.title: Trade Link Posts Record 2025 EPS of NT$46.57
medium.summary: Trade Link-KY reported Q4 2025 net profit of NT$2.74 billion and full-year earnings of NT$9.005 billion (+105% YoY), delivering EPS of NT$46.57 versus NT$25.41 in 2024. January 2026 EPS reached NT$5.01. High-performance computing segment exceeded 45% of revenue mix, driving the record profitability amid continued AI acceleration phase.
